-- rawlen for long strings
assert(rawlen(string.rep('a', 1000)) == 1000)


t = {}
t.__lt = function (a,b,c)
  collectgarbage()
  assert(c == nil)
  if type(a) == 'table' then a = a.x end
  if type(b) == 'table' then b = b.x end
 return a<b, "dummy"
end

t.__le = function (a,b,c)
  assert(c == nil)
  if type(a) == 'table' then a = a.x end
  if type(b) == 'table' then b = b.x end
 return a<=b, "dummy"
end

t.__eq = function (a,b,c)
  assert(c == nil)
  if type(a) == 'table' then a = a.x end
  if type(b) == 'table' then b = b.x end
 return a == b, "dummy"
end

function Op(x) return setmetatable({x=x}, t) end

local function test (a, b, c)
  assert(not(Op(1)<Op(1)) and (Op(1)<Op(2)) and not(Op(2)<Op(1)))
  assert(not(1 < Op(1)) and (Op(1) < 2) and not(2 < Op(1)))
  assert(not(Op('a')<Op('a')) and (Op('a')<Op('b')) and not(Op('b')<Op('a')))
  assert(not('a' < Op('a')) and (Op('a') < 'b') and not(Op('b') < Op('a')))
  assert((Op(1)<=Op(1)) and (Op(1)<=Op(2)) and not(Op(2)<=Op(1)))
  assert((Op('a')<=Op('a')) and (Op('a')<=Op('b')) and not(Op('b')<=Op('a')))
  assert(not(Op(1)>Op(1)) and not(Op(1)>Op(2)) and (Op(2)>Op(1)))
  assert(not(Op('a')>Op('a')) and not(Op('a')>Op('b')) and (Op('b')>Op('a')))
  assert((Op(1)>=Op(1)) and not(Op(1)>=Op(2)) and (Op(2)>=Op(1)))
  assert((1 >= Op(1)) and not(1 >= Op(2)) and (Op(2) >= 1))
  assert((Op('a')>=Op('a')) and not(Op('a')>=Op('b')) and (Op('b')>=Op('a')))
  assert(('a' >= Op('a')) and not(Op('a') >= 'b') and (Op('b') >= Op('a')))
  assert(Op(1) == Op(1) and Op(1) ~= Op(2))
  assert(Op('a') == Op('a') and Op('a') ~= Op('b'))
  assert(a == a and a ~= b)
  assert(Op(3) == c)
end

test(Op(1), Op(2), Op(3))


-- test `partial order'

local function rawSet(x)
  local y = {}
  for _,k in pairs(x) do y[k] = 1 end
  return y
end

local function Set(x)
  return setmetatable(rawSet(x), t)
end

t.__lt = function (a,b)
  for k in pairs(a) do
    if not b[k] then return false end
    b[k] = undef
  end
  return next(b) ~= nil
end

t.__le = function (a,b)
  for k in pairs(a) do
    if not b[k] then return false end
  end
  return true
end

assert(Set{1,2,3} < Set{1,2,3,4})
assert(not(Set{1,2,3,4} < Set{1,2,3,4}))
assert((Set{1,2,3,4} <= Set{1,2,3,4}))
assert((Set{1,2,3,4} >= Set{1,2,3,4}))
assert(not (Set{1,3} <= Set{3,5}))
assert(not(Set{1,3} <= Set{3,5}))
assert(not(Set{1,3} >= Set{3,5}))


t.__eq = function (a,b)
  for k in pairs(a) do
    if not b[k] then return false end
    b[k] = undef
  end
  return next(b) == nil
end

local s = Set{1,3,5}
assert(s == Set{3,5,1})
assert(not rawequal(s, Set{3,5,1}))
assert(rawequal(s, s))
assert(Set{1,3,5,1} == rawSet{3,5,1})

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

t.__eq = function (a,b,c)
  assert(c == nil)
  if type(a) == 'table' then a = a.x end
  if type(b) == 'table' then b = b.x end
 return a == b, "dummy"
end

function Op(x) return setmetatable({x=x}, t) end

local function test (a, b, c)
  assert(not(Op(1)<Op(1)) and (Op(1)<Op(2)) and not(Op(2)<Op(1)))
  assert(not(1 < Op(1)) and (Op(1) < 2) and not(2 < Op(1)))
  assert(not(Op('a')<Op('a')) and (Op('a')<Op('b')) and not(Op('b')<Op('a')))
  assert(not('a' < Op('a')) and (Op('a') < 'b') and not(Op('b') < Op('a')))
  assert((Op(1)<=Op(1)) and (Op(1)<=Op(2)) and not(Op(2)<=Op(1)))
  assert((Op('a')<=Op('a')) and (Op('a')<=Op('b')) and not(Op('b')<=Op('a')))
  assert(not(Op(1)>Op(1)) and not(Op(1)>Op(2)) and (Op(2)>Op(1)))
  assert(not(Op('a')>Op('a')) and not(Op('a')>Op('b')) and (Op('b')>Op('a')))
  assert((Op(1)>=Op(1)) and not(Op(1)>=Op(2)) and (Op(2)>=Op(1)))
  assert((1 >= Op(1)) and not(1 >= Op(2)) and (Op(2) >= 1))
  assert((Op('a')>=Op('a')) and not(Op('a')>=Op('b')) and (Op('b')>=Op('a')))
  assert(('a' >= Op('a')) and not(Op('a') >= 'b') and (Op('b') >= Op('a')))
  assert(Op(1) == Op(1) and Op(1) ~= Op(2))
  assert(Op('a') == Op('a') and Op('a') ~= Op('b'))
  assert(a == a and a ~= b)

content.content:
670	///
671	/// Returns `true` if the metamethod returned a truthy value.
672	/// Raises `LuaError::order_error` if neither operand has the metamethod.
673	///
674	/// PORT NOTE: The `LUA_COMPAT_LT_LE` block (which falls back from `__le` to
675	/// `!(p2 < p1)`) is omitted per PORTING.md §13 — no compatibility shims.
676	pub(crate) fn call_order_tm(
677	    state: &mut LuaState,
678	    p1: &LuaValue,
679	    p2: &LuaValue,
680	    event: TagMethod,
681	) -> Result<bool, LuaError> {
682	    // C: if (callbinTM(L, p1, p2, L->top.p, event))  /* try original event */
683	    //      return !l_isfalse(s2v(L->top.p));
684	    //
685	    // PORT NOTE: In C, `L->top.p` is used as a scratch slot (written by
686	    // callTMres then immediately read) in the EXTRA_STACK reserved area above
687	    // the official stack top — the stack top is NOT officially advanced.
688	    // In Rust we pass `state.top_idx()` as `res`; call_bin_tm → call_tm_res
689	    // pushes 3 values, calls, pops the result back to res, and leaves top ==
690	    // res (i.e. top unchanged relative to entry).  Reading get_at(res_idx)
691	    // after this is safe because the slot was just written and top == res_idx.
692	    //
693	    // TODO(port): Verify in Phase B that no call path between call_bin_tm
694	    // returning and the get_at read can disturb the scratch slot or reset top
695	    // below res_idx.  The invariant holds as long as do_call with 1 result
696	    // leaves exactly one value on the stack above func.
697	    let res_idx = state.top_idx();
698	    if call_bin_tm(state, p1, p2, res_idx, event)? {
699	        // C: return !l_isfalse(s2v(L->top.p));
700	        // l_isfalse(o) → matches!(o, LuaValue::Nil | LuaValue::Bool(false))
701	        let result = state.get_at(res_idx).clone();
702	        return Ok(!matches!(result, LuaValue::Nil | LuaValue::Bool(false)));
703	    }
704	
705	    // PORT NOTE: LUA_COMPAT_LT_LE block skipped (see above).
706	
707	    // C: luaG_ordererror(L, p1, p2);  /* no metamethod found */
708	    Err(LuaError::order_error(p1, p2))
709	}
710	
711	// ── luaT_callorderiTM ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
712	
713	// C: int luaT_callorderiTM (lua_State *L, const TValue *p1, int v2,
714	//                            int flip, int isfloat, TMS event)
715	/// Call an order metamethod where the second operand is a primitive int or float.
716	///
717	/// `v2` is a C `int`; `isfloat` selects whether it is coerced to
718	/// `LuaValue::Float` (via `cast_num`) or kept as `LuaValue::Int`.
719	/// When `flip` is true the operands are swapped so that `p1` was originally
720	/// on the right-hand side.
721	pub(crate) fn call_orderi_tm(
722	    state: &mut LuaState,
723	    p1: &LuaValue,
724	    v2: i32,
725	    flip: bool,
726	    isfloat: bool,
727	    event: TagMethod,
728	) -> Result<bool, LuaError> {
729	    // C: TValue aux; const TValue *p2;
730	    // C: if (isfloat) {
731	    //      setfltvalue(&aux, cast_num(v2));
732	    //    }
733	    //    else
734	    //      setivalue(&aux, v2);
735	    let aux = if isfloat {
736	        // C: cast_num(v2)  →  v2 as f64
737	        LuaValue::Float(v2 as f64)
738	    } else {
739	        LuaValue::Int(v2 as i64)
740	    };
741	
742	    // C: if (flip) {  /* arguments were exchanged? */
743	    //      p2 = p1; p1 = &aux;  /* correct them */
744	    //    }
745	    //    else
746	    //      p2 = &aux;
747	    // C: return luaT_callorderTM(L, p1, p2, event);
748	    if flip {
749	        call_order_tm(state, &aux, p1, event)
750	    } else {
751	        call_order_tm(state, p1, &aux, event)
752	    }
753	}

758	//                              const Proto *p)
759	/// Adjust the stack layout for a vararg function at call entry.
760	///
761	/// Moves the fixed parameters above the extra (vararg) arguments and copies
762	/// the function object alongside them so the function body sees its registers
763	/// at the expected offsets.  Records the extra-argument count in the CallInfo
764	/// for `OP_VARARG` use.
765	///
766	/// Before call:  `[func | fixed... | extra...]`
767	/// After call:   `[func | nil... | extra... | func′ | fixed...]`
768	/// (`ci.func` and `ci.top` are advanced by `actual + 1`.)

delta.text: src/vm.rs:931` (and `:933`) was passing the *table operand itself* into `state.fast_tm_table(Some(h1), T
agMethod::Eq)`. But `fast_tm_table` expects the **metatable**, not the table — it does `mt.get_short_str("
__eq")` on whatever you hand it. So when two distinct tables share a metatable with `__eq`
(e.g. `Op(1) == Op(1)`), both lookups returned `Nil` and `equal_obj` reported `Ok(false)`.
The user-visible failure landed on `assert(Op(1) == Op(1) and Op(1) ~= Op(2))` (combined line 251 /

events.lua line 242).

Compare with the userdata branch (`fast_tm_ud`), which correctly does

`u.metatable()` internally — that's why only the Table case was broken.

## Fix

In `crates/lua-vm/
src/vm.rs:925-946`, extract each operand's metatable first (`h1.metatable()` / `h2.metatable
()`) and pass those `Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>`s into `fast_tm_table`. This matches the C `fasttm(L

, hvalue(t)->metatable, TM_EQ)` shape.

## State after fix

- `cargo build

-p lua-cli` compiles cleanly (warnings only, no errors).
- No temporary instrumentation was added; nothing to remove.
-
Per instructions, I did not re-run the program; the mega-loop will re-test. The next failure inside `events
.lua` (if any) will be a separate bug downstream of this one.

content.text:
## Root cause

`equal_obj` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:931` (and `:933`) was passing the *table operand itself* into `state.fast_tm_table(Some(h1), TagMethod::Eq)`. But `fast_tm_table` expects the **metatable**, not the table — it does `mt.get_short_str("__eq")` on whatever you hand it. So when two distinct tables share a metatable with `__eq` (e.g. `Op(1) == Op(1)`), both lookups returned `Nil` and `equal_obj` reported `Ok(false)`. The user-visible failure landed on `assert(Op(1) == Op(1) and Op(1) ~= Op(2))` (combined line 251 / events.lua line 242).

Compare with the userdata branch (`fast_tm_ud`), which correctly does `u.metatable()` internally — that's why only the Table case was broken.

## Fix

In `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:925-946`, extract each operand's metatable first (`h1.metatable()` / `h2.metatable()`) and pass those `Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>`s into `fast_tm_table`. This matches the C `fasttm(L, hvalue(t)->metatable, TM_EQ)` shape.

## State after fix

- `cargo build -p lua-cli` compiles cleanly (warnings only, no errors).
- No temporary instrumentation was added; nothing to remove.
- Per instructions, I did not re-run the program; the mega-loop will re-test. The next failure inside `events.lua` (if any) will be a separate bug downstream of this one.

result:
## Root cause

`equal_obj` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:931` (and `:933`) was passing the *table operand itself* into `state.fast_tm_table(Some(h1), TagMethod::Eq)`. But `fast_tm_table` expects the **metatable**, not the table — it does `mt.get_short_str("__eq")` on whatever you hand it. So when two distinct tables share a metatable with `__eq` (e.g. `Op(1) == Op(1)`), both lookups returned `Nil` and `equal_obj` reported `Ok(false)`. The user-visible failure landed on `assert(Op(1) == Op(1) and Op(1) ~= Op(2))` (combined line 251 / events.lua line 242).

Compare with the userdata branch (`fast_tm_ud`), which correctly does `u.metatable()` internally — that's why only the Table case was broken.

## Fix

In `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:925-946`, extract each operand's metatable first (`h1.metatable()` / `h2.metatable()`) and pass those `Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>`s into `fast_tm_table`. This matches the C `fasttm(L, hvalue(t)->metatable, TM_EQ)` shape.

## State after fix

- `cargo build -p lua-cli` compiles cleanly (warnings only, no errors).
- No temporary instrumentation was added; nothing to remove.
- Per instructions, I did not re-run the program; the mega-loop will re-test. The next failure inside `events.lua` (if any) will be a separate bug downstream of this one.
